description: Curated vocabulary of intent tags used in component schemas. Agents reason about components by these intents rather than by name, so changes here are breaking.

intents.primary-action: The dominant action in a given context (e.g. submit, confirm).
intents.destructive-action: An action with irreversible or data-losing consequences.
intents.toggle-state: Toggles a binary state without navigating or submitting.
intents.navigation: Moves the user to a different view, section, or URL.
intents.selection: Lets the user pick one item from a set.
intents.form-field: Collects a single value from the user as part of a form.
intents.text-entry: A form field specifically for free-form text.
intents.error: Communicates a failure or invalid state.
intents.container: Groups related content without imposing meaning.
intents.surface: A raised or bordered region that frames content (card, panel).
intents.layout: Arranges other components spatially (tab list, grid).
intents.header: A titled heading region inside a surface.
intents.footer: An actions or summary region inside a surface.
intents.overlay: Content that renders on top of the page (dialog, popover).
intents.modal: An overlay that blocks interaction with the rest of the page.
intents.menu: A list of actions triggered from an anchor element.
intents.identity: Represents a person or entity (avatar, name chip).
intents.labeling: Short label or tag attached to other content.
intents.tabs: Switches between sibling panels using a row of triggers.
intents.dismissible: Can be closed or hidden by the user.
